translated: Awia, when he was about to sacrifice Ishmael at the place called Mahalaf, which is Nɛbayɔf niuhambie, where Abraham was to sacrifice Ishmael, he bound him and laid him down. Jacob went to Bethel

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: A baal ŋɔ, “Filaŋkeŋ i sɔŋ waa kaa di Jekɔb. Ba daŋ jaa ra yirii Izrɛl, dikuubɛɛwiai, i ni Kɔraawiizi ani baala yoowoo, ka ba waa wette bá kpeglii.”
translated: And the children said, “Let us send for Jacob. When he comes to the house of Israel, the elders, the Korahites and the other children will come, and we will go and bury our father.”

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “Nabɔma, akai, a nuu n be. N tɛibuu a bii bɔɔ ani a de hɛri maa. N nɛra sibɔɔrɛ n kpa de di ra tɛi. La ga kpa i lalii hiŋ di ga hug.”
translated: My father, come, your mother is here. I have brought your child and the goat. I have also brought the sheep to give to you. We will go and see your grandmother and then return.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: Awia, if you go, you will die. If you stay, they will come and meet you at home, that is better than going.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The child, when his mother went to the farm and left him at home, he took his father's bow and went to shoot birds. His mother came and met him shooting birds.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: If you have a good friend, you will not lack anything. If you have a bad friend, he will cause you great trouble.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: The people of Zilpa also gave birth to children for Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to his wives, “Get ready, for I have sent for you to come!” Then he took his children and left for Mahanam.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Awia, u ta newea, ba liu. U ta baabuu ba du u himmaa duula, di ɛɛ baal ni haaŋ.
translated: Awia, if you go, you will die. If you go to the bush and you don't have medicine, you will surely die.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Awia, nyiŋŋi daananaa di kuari muugberzenzeŋ bɛl, í wer di ra zuu u biini lugɔ dia. Nyiŋŋi kotaal ham u biini ni u harbɔɔ maa nɛnnɛ.
translated: The man, when he went to the farm to harvest beans, he saw a child lying on the ground. He picked up the child and carried him to his house.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N daŋ tɛi nannakpila werge, baŋhina daŋ ɛɛ koorizenaa. I daŋ dɔɔ nannakpila werge, guŋ gil bá birgi hɛrkpila hina.
translated: If you have a good friend, you will not lack anything. If you have a bad friend, he will lead you into trouble.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Denii Zilpa kaa lul biibaal tɛɛ Jekɔb.
translated: The children of Zilpah were born to Jacob.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Jekɔb n wa na baa u ŋɔ, “Kɔraawiizi ni u mɛlikazi chɔɔllɛ keŋ!” U faa dau de sɔŋ Mahanam.
translated: Jacob said to his sons, “Why have you brought trouble on me by making the man think you have another brother?” They replied, “The man questioned us closely about ourselves and our family.”
